question: Does the model explicitly answer both sub-questions (1) and (2) in its final response?
Does the model correctly conclude that AC is parallel to BD (AC ∥ BD)?
Does the model calculate the vertical height of the clothes rack (distance from AC to ground) as 120 cm?
Does the model conclude that the windbreaker will touch the ground based on the comparison (122 cm > 120 cm)?
Did the model correctly calculate the lengths of OB and OD as 85 cm (derived from 136 - 51)?
Did the model identify the correct ratio (e.g., OA/OB = 3/5) or proportional relationship to establish similarity between △AOC and △BOD?
Did the model explicitly identify that ∠AOC and ∠BOD are vertical angles (or equal) to support the similarity proof?
Did the model use a valid geometric criterion (e.g., SAS Similarity leading to alternate interior angles) to prove AC ∥ BD?
Did the model correctly identify that triangle OEF is isosceles and that the altitude from O bisects the base (calculating EM = 16 cm)?
Did the model correctly calculate the length of OM as 30 cm using the Pythagorean theorem (√(34² - 16²))?
Did the model correctly set up a proportion based on similar triangles (e.g., △OEM ∽ △ABH) or a scaling factor to determine the total height?
Did the model correctly compare the windbreaker length (122 cm) with the calculated rack height (120 cm) to justify the final conclusion?
If perpendicular lines are depicted (e.g., OM ⊥ EF), do they visually appear to meet at 90-degree angles or include right-angle markers?
Are the text labels (A, B, C, D, O, E, F) in the generated image(s) legible and placed near their corresponding geometric points?
Are the key geometric points positioned correctly relative to each other: O at the intersection, A/C at the top, and B/D on the ground?
Is the chain buckle EF depicted as a horizontal segment connecting the legs below point O?
If auxiliary point M is shown, is it visually positioned as the midpoint of segment EF?
If multiple images are generated, do the labels and relative positions of the rack components remain consistent across all images?
If the text describes specific auxiliary constructions (e.g., 'draw OM perpendicular to EF'), are these constructions present in the generated image?
Does the visual depiction of AC and BD align with the textual conclusion of parallelism (i.e., they appear parallel rather than converging)?
Does the generated image avoid contradictions with the text (e.g., labels swapping positions or conflicting length depictions)?
